overview.short: Sacred Heart French Cemetery is a Roman Catholic cemetery located at Laldighi in Chandannagar, West Bengal, which is associated with the Sacred Heart Church.

topPlaces.name: Krishna Bhabini Nari Siksha Mandir
topPlaces.description: Krishna Bhabini Nari Siksha Mandir is a well-known girls school in Chandannagar, West Bengal, India. It was established in 1926 by Harihar Sett in the name of his mother Krishnabhabini Das.

topPlaces.name: Kanailal Vidyamandir
topPlaces.description: Kanailal Vidyamandir is one of the oldest schools in Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India. Established in 1862, the school is located at Grand Trunk Road in Chandannagar.

topPlaces.name: Sacred Heart Church, Chandannagar
topPlaces.description: The Sacred Heart Church (French: Église du Sacré-Cœur, pronounced [e.ɡliz dy sakʁe kœʁ]; pronounced [ˈpobit̪ːɾoˑ ˈɦɾid̪ɔe̯ ɡiɾdʒaˑ]) is a Roman Catholic heritage church situated at Chandannagar in Hooghly district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It is under the Archdiocese of Calcutta, and it is a heritage site declared by the West Bengal Heritage Commission.

topPlaces.name: Chandannagar
topPlaces.description: Chandannagar (Bengali pronunciation: [t͡ʃɔn.d̪ɔn.nɔ.ɡoɾ]), also known by its former names Chandernagor (French pronunciation: [ʃɑ̃.dɛʁ.na.ɡɔʁ]) and Chandernagore, is a city in the Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is headquarter of the Chandannagore subdivision and is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

topPlaces.name: St Joseph's Convent, Chandannagar
topPlaces.description: St Joseph's Convent (SJC) is a Catholic all-girls school in Chandannagar, established in 1861 by the Sisters of St. Joseph of Cluny in the Archdiocese of Calcutta.

topPlaces.name: Sri Aurobindo Vidyamandir, Chandannagar
topPlaces.description: Sri Aurobindo Vidyamandir is an English medium school in Hatkhola, Chandannagar, West Bengal, India established in 1973.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Chandannagar
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Chandannagar was a successful attack on the French Indies Company settlement of Chandernagore by a British force under the command of Robert Clive and Charles Watson on 23 March 1757 during the Seven Years' War and the Third Carnatic war. Chandernagore remained under military occupation by the British until the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763, which brought an end to the war.

topPlaces.name: Jagatdal
topPlaces.description: Jagatdal (also known as Jagaddal) is a locality in Bhatpara of North 24 Parganas district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Krishna Bhabini Nari Siksha Mandir & around

items.note: Krishna Bhabini Nari Siksha Mandir is a well-known girls school in Chandannagar, West Bengal, India. It was established in 1926 by Harihar Sett in the name of his mother Krishnabhabini Das.
Kanailal Vidyamandir is one of the oldest schools in Hooghly district in the state of West Bengal, India. Established in 1862, the school is located at Grand Trunk Road in Chandannagar.

The Sacred Heart Church (French: Église du Sacré-Cœur, pronounced [e.ɡliz dy sakʁe kœʁ]; pronounced [ˈpobit̪ːɾoˑ ˈɦɾid̪ɔe̯ ɡiɾdʒaˑ]) is a Roman Catholic heritage church situated at Chandannagar in Hooghly district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It is under the Archdiocese of Calcutta, and it is a heritage site declared by the West Bengal Heritage Commission.

Chandannagar (Bengali pronunciation: [t͡ʃɔn.d̪ɔn.nɔ.ɡoɾ]), also known by its former names Chandernagor (French pronunciation: [ʃɑ̃.dɛʁ.na.ɡɔʁ]) and Chandernagore, is a city in the Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is headquarter of the Chandannagore subdivision and is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).
St Joseph's Convent (SJC) is a Catholic all-girls school in Chandannagar, established in 1861 by the Sisters of St. Joseph of Cluny in the Archdiocese of Calcutta.
Sri Aurobindo Vidyamandir is an English medium school in Hatkhola, Chandannagar, West Bengal, India established in 1973.

The Battle of Chandannagar was a successful attack on the French Indies Company settlement of Chandernagore by a British force under the command of Robert Clive and Charles Watson on 23 March 1757 during the Seven Years' War and the Third Carnatic war. Chandernagore remained under military occupation by the British until the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763, which brought an end to the war.

Jagatdal (also known as Jagaddal) is a locality in Bhatpara of North 24 Parganas district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sacred Heart French Cemetery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sacred Heart French Cemetery is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sacred Heart French Cemetery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sacred Heart French Cemetery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sacred Heart French Cemetery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sacred Heart French Cemetery?
faq.a: Stays in Sacred Heart French Cemetery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sacred Heart French Cemetery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Krishna Bhabini Nari Siksha Mandir, Kanailal Vidyamandir, Sacred Heart Church, Chandannagar, Chandannagar, St Joseph's Convent, Chandannagar.
